title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer requests three changes: (1) in the Keystone Dockerfile, combine the local source path with its extras into a single `uv pip install` argument (`"/tmp/keystone[ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1]"`) instead of passing them separately, to avoid ambiguous PyPI resolution; (2) in the constraint overrides test, add `assert_file_not_contains` checks in Test 5 to verify that old `cryptography` and `keystoneauth1` versions are actually removed, matching the pattern already used in Test 2; (3) in `apply-constraint-overrides.sh`, add a pre-flight existence check for `$CONSTRAINTS` (mirroring the existing `$OVERRIDES` guard) so the script fails with a clear error instead of a cryptic `sed` message when run from the wrong directory.

code_comments.body:
**Extras should be co-located with the local source path**

`/tmp/keystone` (local source) and `"keystone[ldap]"` etc. (PyPI references with extras) are passed as separate, independent arguments to `uv pip install`. While uv/pip correctly de-duplicates these in practice — recognising that `keystone` is already satisfied by the local path and only installing the extra dependencies — the conventional way to install a local package with extras is to reference the path directly with brackets:

```suggestion
    uv pip install --prefix /var/lib/openstack \
        --constraint /tmp/upper-constraints.txt \
        "/tmp/keystone[ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1]"
```

The single-argument form leaves no room for ambiguity: uv cannot inadvertently resolve `keystone[ldap]` from PyPI if the local source is unexpectedly absent or if the version does not satisfy the constraint at resolution time. It also aligns with the standard `pip install /path/to/package[extra]` idiom documented in PEP 508.

The single-argument form leaves no room for ambiguity: uv cannot inadvertently resolve `keystone[ldap]` from PyPI if the local source is unexpectedly absent or if the version does not satisfy the constraint at resolution time. It also aligns with the standard `pip install /path/to/package[extra]` idiom documented in PEP 508.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Fixed: consolidated the separate /tmp/keystone and individual "keystone[ldap]" etc. arguments into a single "/tmp/keystone[ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1]" argument on line 16 of the Dockerfile, exactly matching the reviewer's suggestion.

code_comments.body:
**Test 5 missing `assert_file_not_contains` for replaced versions**

Test 2 (single replacement) asserts both that the new value is present *and* that the old value is absent. Test 5 (multiple overrides) only checks for the new values' presence and `oslo.messaging`'s removal — it does not verify that the old `cryptography===44.0.0` and `keystoneauth1===5.10.0` entries were actually deleted before the new lines were appended.

Without these negative assertions, a bug where `sed -i` silently fails to delete existing pins would not be caught by this test (the `assert_file_contains` checks would still pass as long as the new line was appended, even if the old one remained, creating a duplicate).

```suggestion
  assert_file_contains "cryptography updated" "$workdir/upper-constraints.txt" "^cryptography===44.0.1$"
  assert_file_not_contains "old cryptography removed" "$workdir/upper-constraints.txt" "cryptography===44.0.0"
  assert_file_not_contains "oslo.messaging removed" "$workdir/upper-constraints.txt" "oslo.messaging"
  assert_file_contains "keystoneauth1 updated" "$workdir/upper-constraints.txt" "^keystoneauth1===5.11.0$"
  assert_file_not_contains "old keystoneauth1 removed" "$workdir/upper-constraints.txt" "keystoneauth1===5.10.0"
  assert_file_contains "oslo.config untouched" "$workdir/upper-constraints.txt" "^oslo.config===9.7.0$"
```

code_comments.resolution: Fixed: added assert_file_not_contains checks for "cryptography===44.0.0" (line 129) and "keystoneauth1===5.10.0" (line 132) in Test 5, interleaved with the existing positive assertions, exactly matching the reviewer's suggested ordering.

code_comments.body:
**Missing pre-flight validation for `upper-constraints.txt`**

`CONSTRAINTS` is a bare filename with no path. If the script is invoked from any directory other than the repo root, `sed -i` will fail with a cryptic error like `sed: can't read upper-constraints.txt: No such file or directory` rather than a message pointing the user to the root cause.

Adding an explicit existence check immediately after the `OVERRIDES` variable gives a much clearer failure:

```bash
if [ ! -f "$CONSTRAINTS" ]; then
  echo "Error: '$CONSTRAINTS' not found. Run this script from the repository root." >&2
  exit 1
fi
```

A similar guard already exists for `$OVERRIDES` (line 19). Applying the same pattern to `$CONSTRAINTS` makes the script's preconditions symmetric and easier to diagnose.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Fixed: added a pre-flight existence check for $CONSTRAINTS (lines 19-22) immediately after the OVERRIDES variable assignment, following the same pattern as the existing $OVERRIDES guard, with a clear error message directing the user to run from the repository root.

resolution_summary: All three review comments were addressed exactly as suggested. The Dockerfile was updated to co-locate extras with the local source path in a single argument, the constraint override script gained a pre-flight validation check for upper-constraints.txt, and Test 5 was strengthened with assert_file_not_contains checks for replaced versions. No changes were made to files or locations beyond those directly mentioned in the review feedback.
